calendar.matters:
ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Aug-29-2017 Off Calendar

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jul-18-2017 Continued To Sep-19-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-25-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Reno FR Fernandez, III, Esq. (415-362-2220) of MacDonald Fernandez, LLP for Defendant Wing K. Chan through Court Call.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is uncontested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT WING CHAN'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT FOR DAMAGES as to to all causes of action in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff Peter Kutrubes is sustained as to all causes of action. No opposition filed. Mr. Chan's request for sanctions is denied because the request was not included in a notice of motion and was only referred to in the memorandum. Order is signed. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. =(302/HK)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-25-2017 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT WING CHAN'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT - Defendant Wing Chan's demurrer to both causes of action in the complaint filed by plaintiff Peter Kutrubes is sustained without leave to amend as to the first cause of action for account stated and sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to the second cause of action for breach of contract. Mr. Chan correctly cites the rule, recently reaffirmed in Professional Collection Consultants v. Lauron (2017) 8 Cal. App. 5th 958, 966, that the four year statute of limitations on a cause of action for account stated is triggered by the last item on the account. Since Mr. Kutrubes' opposition papers show that the last item on the account was more than four years before this case was filed and Mr. Kutrubes offers no explanation how he can avoid the statute of limitations on that claim, the demurrer to the first cause of action is sustained without leave to amend. While the allegations in paragraph 12 of the complaint show that the second cause of action for breach of contract is also time barred, Mr. Kutrubes has stated facts in his opposition papers that may show that the breach of contract claim is not time barred. If he can do so in good faith and adequately explain any real or apparent inconsistencies between the allegations in the first amended complaint with those in the complaint, Mr. Kutrubes is given leave to file a first amended complaint alleging a non-time barred claim for breach of contract. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR # 8189 =302/hk

MOTION TO DISMISS FOR FAILURE TO PROSCECUTE
